name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: Day 80 (Monday). **US 10Y 4.59% (carries forward Friday close, +3.0% cumulative Fri+Mon, +6.6% 1M, +13.4% 3M); US 2Y 3.59% flat; US 30Y ~5.12% (flat-to-up vs Friday, 10-month high)** per markets dossier Section A. Bloomberg 'worst weekly Treasury rout in a year' framing carries from Friday. **Primary chain (~70% rates regime)**: April PPI +1.4% m/m (BLS May 13, biggest since Dec 2022) + Warsh effective May 16 (rate-hike-before-year-end now in CNBC/Yahoo discussion) — Fed easing path closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027; rate-cut bets now sit beyond 2026. **Secondary chain (~30% crisis)**: Brent $111.47 +2.0% Monday (StanChart $130-145 path activates same day) → oil pass-through to PPI intensifies → producer-side inflation feed-through to Treasury yields. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.05-7.10% flat** (+6-8 bp 1M, +13 bp 3M); investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; benchmark 6.48% 2035 bond yield ~7.05%; FII debt outflows continuing. **Cross-asset stagflation regime intact**: 10Y +14 bp Friday + DXY 99.22 firm + Gold -0.4% (real-yield channel competing) + VIX 18.43 sub-20 + S&P -1.24% Friday. **Tuesday May 19 NSC binary**: strike orders → yields gap higher again (energy pass-through intensifies); back-off → yields hold or modestly retrace; nothing operational → yields oscillate. **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE YIELDS ARE FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSES: US 10Y 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025); US 30Y 5.121% (highest since May 2025).** Per markets dossier Section A: India 10Y G-Sec 7.09% (+5 bps 1D May 15) — rebound from prior decline; CCIL monthly avg 6.95% vs 7.04% prior; RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15. **STAGFLATION REGIME UNCHANGED**: April PPI +1.4% m/m + April CPI 3.8% YoY closed Fed easing path indefinitely; Warsh effective May 16. **Monday May 18 open is the test** — if NYT preparation report is fully priced into Brent and oil gaps higher, yields move higher again; if markets discount the report as posturing, yields hold. **Tertiary chain (~15% Iran) intensifies**: Trump 2-month ultimatum letter + NYT preparation report adds tail-risk premium. Reversibility unchanged: crisis component (~15%) reverses if Iran responds within 2-month window; PPI/CPI inflation regime (~60%) does NOT reverse — structural through Q3 2026 at minimum; Warsh transition (~25%) takes ~90 days to clarify; risk asymmetric to hawkish surprise. **PRIOR (May 16):** STAGFLATION BOND ROUT — US 10Y +14bp to 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025); US 30Y +11bp to 5.121% (highest since May 2025) — Bloomberg labelled it 'the worst weekly Treasury rout in a year.'** Primary chain (~60% inflation): **April PPI +1.4% m/m / +6% YoY (BLS May 13)** confirms producer-side inflation has fully absorbed the energy shock — Core PPI +1% (vs 0.4% est), ~60% of rise was in services, energy at the root; combined with April CPI 3.8% YoY (May 12) Fed easing path is closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027 earlier in the week; CNBC/Yahoo reporting markets now consider rate hike possible. Secondary chain (~25% Fed transition): **Warsh confirmed 54-45 May 13 replacing Powell effective today May 16**; Warsh's preference for trimmed mean inflation measures = sees underlying inflation cooler than headline, but Warsh shrinks balance sheet + relies on rate tool = potentially more hawkish on QE channel; markets uncertain about reaction function for first 90 days = term premium expanding. Tertiary chain (~15% Iran): Trump 2-month ultimatum letter creates a defined uncertainty window; oil pass-through to PPI documented and ongoing. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (flat-to-easing)**: traders sidelined ahead of Trump-Xi summit; RBI bond-tax cut for foreigners under consideration; RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15. **Cross-asset stagflation confirmation**: 10Y +14bp / 30Y +11bp / DXY +0.4% / Silver -9.1% / Gold -2.6% / S&P -1.24% / VIX 17.26→18.43 / WTI +4.2%. The paper-equity leg of the cycle-extreme bifurcation reversed within 24 hours toward crisis confirmation. PRIOR (May 15): **US 10Y 4.46% MAY 15 (-0.4% 1D, +4.2% 1M, +10.1% 3M); US 2Y 3.59% (-0.3% 1D, -0.7% 1M, -0.1% 3M).** Front-end says no near-term cut, no near-term hike either. **April CPI 3.8% YoY + April PPI +1.4% m/m closed near-term Fed easing door**; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. Real-yield channel keeps gold in regime-shift unwind (gold -2.2% 1D); DXY 99.13 (+0.3% 1D) — modest dollar bid on Trump-Xi joint statement. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-regime + paper-physical bifurcation at extreme**: VIX 17.26 cycle low + S&P/NASDAQ/Dow record closes + 10Y at 4.46% holding all reinforce paper-side complacency on a day with two new kinetic incidents and an Israeli KIA. India 10Y G-Sec 7.12% (DealPlexus / CCIL May 14 — yield easing as traders awaited Trump-Xi summit outcome; CCIL monthly avg 6.95% vs 7.04% prior). **US 10Y 4.48% MAY 14 (+0.4% 1D, +5.3% 1M, +10.5% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, -0.3% 1M, +0.2% 3M).** CPI absorption — Fed easing closed (April CPI 3.8% three-year high May 12; energy +17.9% YoY contributed >40% headline gain; core +2.8% YoY). Front-end says no near-term cut, no near-term hike either. **Real-yield channel keeps gold underperforming** ($4,701.50 +0.1% 1D — modestly underperforming bear case on Trump-Xi opening). India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (month high — investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; Moody's sovereign-cut absorbed; +bps vs recent 6.96% range). DXY 98.48 (-0.0% 1D, +0.4% 1M, +1.4% 3M) — dollar failing to bid on crisis day (G5). **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime: VIX 17.87 sub-18 + S&P 500 +0.6% + 10Y at 4.48% holding all reinforce that markets see crisis as INFLATION + FLOW event** (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT financial-system stress event. PRIOR (May 9): **US 10Y 4.36% MAY 8 CLOSE (-0.6% 1D, +1.7% 1M, +4.0% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, +0.2% 1M).** Modest 10Y pullback on stronger-than-expected May 8 jobs print (per Reuters/Investing 'labor market resilience') reduced immediate June-cut probability; longer-end yields easing back from FOMC-driven highs. **Real-yield channel still keeping gold underperforming:** US 10Y at 4.36% kept real yields competitive vs gold; gold $4,720.40 (+0.4% 1D) underperformed on a crisis-day with May 7 destroyer attacks + May 8 second tanker disabling + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' speech. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime:** VIX cycle-low 17.08 + S&P 500 record close 7,398.93 + 10Y not making new highs all reinforce that markets see the West Asia crisis as an INFLATION + FLOW event (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT a financial-system stress event (which would be positive for gold + bid for 10Y). Cheniere $3.5B Q1 GAAP loss on derivative MtM is the closest thing to a financial-stress signal but is firm-specific not systemic. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0% (+6-8 bps cycle, ~+15bps from late-Apr lows, ~+25bps from pre-crisis) — rupee weakness + oil inflation + FII channel keeping pressure. DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M) modest dollar weakness. Powell verbal 'hasn't even peaked yet' on energy inflation still operative; CME FedWatch June-cut probability now low.

current.price: US 10Y 4.595% May 15 (+14 bps 1D, highest since Feb 2025); US 30Y 5.121% (+11 bps, highest since May 2025); US 2Y 4.09% (close-day flat); India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (flat-to-easing)
current.regime: STAGFLATION BOND ROUT. April PPI +1.4% m/m (biggest since Dec 2022) closes Fed easing path. Warsh confirmed May 13 (54-45), effective May 16, replacing Powell — Warsh hawkish on balance sheet/rate-tool. Trump-Iran reversal layered on top. 'Worst weekly Treasury rout in a year' (Bloomberg). Real-yield channel dominant: gold -2.6%, silver -9.1%. DXY +0.4% to 99.27.

recent_signals.headline: **US 10Y 4.62% (+9% 1M) / US 30Y ~5.13% (10-MONTH HIGH, +30bp 3M) — BLOOMBERG 'WORST WEEKLY TREASURY ROUT IN A YEAR' STILL OPERATIVE.** US 2Y 3.57% (-0.6% 1D) — hold cuts priced. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.10-7.13% (flat to +5-7 bp; six-week high on crude + UST). **The rate stress is the cleanest non-crisis driver — does NOT reverse on Hormuz reopening.** Drivers: PPI +1.4% m/m hot, Warsh effective May 16, oil pass-through (partial crisis-link), inflation-persistence framing from Fed officials. S&P/NASDAQ flat-to-down marginally despite postponement = rates overriding geopolitics.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A13; Markets dossier D1 F
recent_signals.verification: 10Y 4.62% / 30Y 5.13% 10-month high = CONFIRMED operational; Bloomberg framing = CONFIRMED; India 10Y ~7.10-7.13% = CONFIRMED (Trading Economics).
